steps.title: Identify where the second independent clause begins
steps.content: The key signal is “An enormous geoglyph of the letter J overlooks Juab High” — a complete independent clause (subject + verb + object). This clause cannot be attached to the previous clause with only a comma.

steps.title: Apply the sentence boundary rule
steps.content: When two independent clauses meet, they must be separated by a period, semicolon, or comma + coordinating conjunction. Option C uses a period to correctly separate “schools that have their own hillside geoglyphs.” from “An enormous geoglyph of the letter J overlooks Juab High, while a geoglyph of the letters PC overlooks Park City High.” The other options violate this rule with inadequate punctuation. That matches choice C — C is correct.
